bio:
Billy has served in a variety of counseling settings and roles since 2014, ranging from the direct provision of counseling teenagers and adults to serving in organizational leadership roles. Through these encounters, he has developed a passion for helping men through the "trenches of life". Specifically, his focus areas include working with those experiencing anxiety, depression and sadness, anger, addictive behavior, relationship and parenting issues, low self-value, and motivation concerns. In the summer of 2025, Billy felt a calling to start Morning Light Counseling and is honored to get to walk alongside those with whom he works with. 

Likes- Walks with his wife, Adventures with his children, Fishing, Vintage Camcorders, Miami Dolphins (Although a trying interest--has allowed him to practice psychological flexibility every Fall!)

Dislikes- Self-promotion, social media
approachBody:
Clients have described Billy as having the ability to "Mercifully confront while at the same time, purposely comfort". He is a believer that God is not only the author of life, but the source of true redemptive change. As such, his counsel flows in alignment with a biblical worldview. He helps clients by assisting in processing their story, identifying sources of pain both self-inflicted and imposed on them by others or by life circumstances and by allowing them to evaluate how their functional worldviews align with their stated values. His approach blends narrative work with the narrative of the Biblical story.

For more information, reach out to him directly by clicking "request appointment" below.
credentials: Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC), Commonwealth of Pennsylvania
Focused practice: counseling for men, ages 12 and up
